statement: If `f` and `g` commute, then `Function.iterate f n` commutes with `g` for all `n`.
lean_statement:
theorem iterate_comp_eq_comp_iterate
    {alpha : Type*} (f g : alpha → alpha) (h : f ∘ g = g ∘ f) :
    ∀ n : Nat, (Nat.iterate f n) ∘ g = g ∘ (Nat.iterate f n)
proof:
Proof: by induction on `n`. For `n = 0`, `iterate f 0` is the identity, so it commutes with `g`.
Assume `(iterate f n) ∘ g = g ∘ (iterate f n)`. Then
`(iterate f (n+1)) ∘ g = (f ∘ iterate f n) ∘ g = f ∘ ((iterate f n) ∘ g)
= f ∘ (g ∘ iterate f n) = (f ∘ g) ∘ iterate f n = (g ∘ f) ∘ iterate f n
= g ∘ (f ∘ iterate f n) = g ∘ (iterate f (n+1))`,
using the commutation `f ∘ g = g ∘ f`. This proves the claim for all `n`.
